title: All circuit and causal claims rest on one 2B base model plus five steering pairs pre-selected for steering success, while the front door generalizes to 'Medical AI'
evidence: Core premises CONFIRMED: patientwords/data/provenance.json steering_titration._ reads verbatim 'boost dose-response + faithfulness on the 5 boost-recovered downgrade cases'; cells recomputed 2.5:4/5, 5:5/5, 10:4/5 (plus 20:1/4), placebo 0/5 -- and provenance.json steering shows the selection base rate (boost_recoveries 5 of n_phrases 20). The translation page tt-notes (translation/index.html:259,264) present '4/5, 5/5, 4/5' and 'the effect is the clinical circuit, not steering itself' with no mention of the boost-recovered selection rule (the 5/20 base rate is disclosed only on simulated-scenarios/index.html:695-697). Quoted page language verified: index.html:311 H1 'Medical AI must be ready to interpret them', :312 'rewriting the sentence in clinical terms restores it', :313 'competing recommendations'; methods.html:288-289 'The advice changed, not just its probability'; translation/index.html:204 'Translation matters most when it changes the advice'; engine CLAUDE.md scope 'measures language-model probabilities only -- nothing here generates or evaluates medical advice'; docs/preregistration_tierB.md:18-20 'causally manipulable with placebo control'; one-model circuit scope confirmed (engine CLAUDE.md; jlens_insights.json model=gemma-2-2b; activation_patching.yml default model gemma-2-2b:42). TWO CORRECTIONS: (1) 'the H1, og tags... do not inherit the caveat' is wrong for the og/meta tags -- index.html:7 meta description and :13 og:description both end 'in gemma-2-2b', translation/index.html:13 og:description says 'Live gemma-2-2b trace', and the lede directly under the H1 (index.html:312) opens 'We trace how gemma-2-2b reads...'; only the H1 itself and the twitter:description (index.html:21) lack model scoping. (2) The rank arm is n=4 measured, not n=5: provenance.json low_rank_6_10 = 3/4 recovered ('one call 500'd server-side'), replicated 3/4.

title: The clinical_mass / feature-tagging construct has zero reported validation and a circular category definition
evidence: All quoted text verified: methods.html:235 'Every feature gets sorted: clinical, off-target, or structural'; :243-245 off-target = 'features dragged in by colloquial wording... These are the mechanism of the language penalty' (circular definition as charged); :251 'the clinical share of the circuit falls and the off-target share rises'; :516-518 limitations bullet 'Both steps can be wrong, and a mislabeled feature shifts the clinical-mass numbers'; wording-differences/index.html:203 'green clinical features stack over the swapped word and feed the continuation; the idiom replaces them'. Tagger mechanism confirmed: medlang_circuits/feature_tagger.py keyword-matches Neuronpedia autointerp descriptions + top activating tokens (keywords.py external JSON vocab); notably DEFAULT_CATEGORY = CATEGORY_OFF_TARGET (feature_tagger.py:36) -- unmatched non-structural features default into the very category claimed to be 'the mechanism', stronger than the report states. No validation artifact found in either repo: searches for inter-rater/precision/recall/agreement/kappa/audit across pages, data/, docs/, scripts/ find only the blind 20-pair STIMULUS QC (docs/stimulus_qc_v1.json -- stimuli, not feature tags) and retrace repeatability (data/retrace_consistency.json -- trace-parameter stability, not tag accuracy); no human-tagged sample, no keyword-vocabulary sensitivity analysis. The untagged-model clinical_mass ~0.0 artifact is documented in engine CLAUDE.md ('an artifact, not a finding') with no analogous analysis for tagged-model numbers.

title: 'Never formed' means 'never entered a top-8 readout window', but the page repeatedly claims nonexistence -- and the instrument's own clinical-side data show the window misses answers that demonstrably exist
evidence: CONFIRMED: top-8 window per jlens_insights.json '_' ('Ranks are within the lens's top-8 readout; never formed means never entered that readout'); MIN_PREFIX_CHARS=4 in engine scripts/jlens_readout.py:108 and prereg_amendment2_depth_DRAFT.md:27-28; recomputed 40 of 170 clinical readings clin_formed=null (formation.clinical_never=40) and exactly 4 'held' pairs with clin_formed=null (plus 2 hijacks); strong existence phrasing in body verified (technical/index.html:238 'Did the right answer ever exist inside the model?', :266 'It is existence.', :287 'the answer is not readable at any layer'). TWO CORRECTIONS: (1) The claim that these 40 targets 'pass the >=0.02 measurability screen' is factually wrong for most: joining engine trace_out batch_summary screening records by (dataset,index), only 7 of the 40 never-formed clinical readings have screening status 'passed' (0.02); 25 were explicitly 'screened_out' (final-layer target probability < 0.02, so the lens not seeing them is expected, not blindness); 7 come from the unscreened urgency_downgrades_20260707T1 set; 1 has no record. The '~24% miss rate on answers known to have measurable probability' (40/170) is therefore unsupported -- on screen-passed readings the never-formed rate is 7/90 (~8%). (2) 'the honest disclosure exists only in the page's endnote' is overstated: window-qualified language appears in the body -- technical/index.html:244 ('the clinical answer never enters the window'), the rendered dd-form-text (JS ~:715, 'the answer never enters the window in 56 of 170...'), and the fig-traj caption (:676, 'rank of the clinical answer in the top-8 window'); the endnote (:602-604) adds the explicit top-8 definition.

title: The capture/hijack taxonomy attributes to wording what is mostly lens blindness: 34 of 56 'captures' are unreadable under BOTH wordings
evidence: Recomputed from patientwords/data/jlens_insights.json points: 56 captures, of which exactly 34 have clin_formed=null (all 34 also pat_formed=null), leaving 22 wording-attributable captures vs 14 hijacks -- the 34/56 and 22-v-14 numbers are exact. Screening join corroborates the 'lens blindness' reading: 20 of the 34 both-null captures were screened_out (<0.02) and 7 unscreened; only 6 passed the screen. The page narrates the uncorrected split: technical/index.html dd-tax-text (JS ~:747-753) reports '56 captures, where the everyday reading owns the trajectory and the clinical answer never appears, against 14 hijacks... Most failure here is capture, not hijack: the wording decides the computation early'. Capture winner lock-in median layer 24 confirmed (taxonomy.capture.winner_lock_in median 24, q75 25; n_layers 26, i.e. layers 0-25) -- though note the same sentence ends '...even though the winning word is not locked in until late', so the early/late tension is acknowledged in-page even if not reconciled. Exemplar trajectory verified exactly: the showcased fig-traj exemplar is the first hijack (pair 12, technical/index.html:649 selects class==='hijack'); its pat_ranks are rank 7 at layer 20, null at 21, rank 8 at 22, rank 5 at 23, null at 24-25. No persistence criterion exists: engine scripts/jlens_insights.py formation_layer (:24-29) returns the first layer with a non-null target_rank, and classify() (:83-96) assigns hijack whenever pat_formed is non-null and pat_final_rank is null -- a single-layer blip counts as formation. (The engine's own critic already flags the 34/56 point -- docs/critic/critic_20260714.md:40 -- but the published page does not.)

title: Translation-page 'restores the prediction / restores the circuit' claims outrun an uncontrolled n=14, and the depth-router story is contradicted by the site's own flat by-class recovery numbers
evidence: Numbers CONFIRMED: provenance.json translation_cases.all.cases = 18 entries, recomputed Counter {recovered:8, already_ok:4, unrecovered:3, worsened:3} => 8/14 classifiable = 57%; jlens_depth.json translation.by_class recomputed exactly: suppressed(lost-late) n=4 mean_recovery 0.157, absent(never-formed) n=10 mean 0.163, retained n=21 mean 0.13 -- flat across classes, never-formed slightly ABOVE lost-late, opposite the H-D1 direction registered in docs/prereg_amendment2_depth_DRAFT.md:36-37 ('mean urgency recovery is greater for lost-late pairs than never-formed pairs'). Page language verified: translation/index.html:202 subtitle 'restores the prediction'; :13 og:description 'restores the circuit'; :224-227 depth claim ('lost late, which translation recovers... never form, which translation supplies'); :222 '41% after translation (clinical baseline 26%)' and :208 '45% in the July 7 study trace, 41% on the July 9 re-trace'; technical/index.html:331 lists the placebo-paraphrase lens comparison under 'Queued next'; prereg (preregistration_tierB.md:46) confirms 'Translation panel: only on flagged downgrades'; methods.html:389 audit line 'translating patient words into clinical words destroys information' appears nowhere on the translation page; circuit-level recovery is quantified nowhere on the site (urgency_shift/provenance publish token probabilities and tier recovery only; the three-panel exemplar carries the circuit claim). CORRECTION on 'There is no control arm': a placebo-paraphrase translation arm has already LANDED in the engine -- trace_out/pairs_20260711T051145Z_txplacebo/ (10 batch_summary parts, screen 0.02, translated = meaning-preserving colloquial paraphrase, e.g. 'My chest is giving me trouble' for 'My chest is acting up') -- it is traced but unanalyzed/unpublished; and provenance.json paraphrase_robustness already publishes an innocuous-paraphrase noise benchmark (mean |delta| 0.0642 vs penalty 0.070). The site-level absence of a published control comparison stands, but 'no control arm' is inaccurate at the artifact level.
